name: Walloon Brabant (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: the sable field of the historic Brabant arms
the lion's tongue and claws and the two Walloon roosters in the corners

description: A black field charged with a yellow lion rampant with red tongue and claws (the Brabant lion); in the upper hoist and upper fly corners, two red Walloon roosters facing each other.
history: Adopted on 2 January 1995, after the 1995 partition of the historic Province of Brabant. The lion is the Brabant lion of the medieval Duchy of Brabant; the two red roosters in the chief recall the Coq hardi of the Walloon Region and mark the province's Walloon identity.

features.role: Brabant lion (Leo Belgicus), rampant lion with red tongue and claws
features.symbolism: the historic lion of the Duchy of Brabant

features.role: two Walloon roosters (Coq hardi) in the upper hoist and upper fly corners
features.symbolism: the rooster of the Walloon Region, identifying the province with French-speaking Wallonia after the 1995 split of the Province of Brabant
